put.summary: Updates an existing annotation on an asset.
put.description: Behavior: Updates the specified annotation's properties including text, timecode, and position. The annotation must exist for the specified asset. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The annotation record is updated. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the annotation is not found.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset containing the annotation.

parameters.name: annonationId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the annotation to update.

requestBody.description: The updated annotation details.

200.description: The annotation was updated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

404.description: The annotation was not found.

delete.summary: Deletes a related asset from a parent asset.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes the association between the parent asset and the specified related asset (e.g., proxy video, subtitle file, thumbnail). The related asset is permanently deleted. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The related asset is permanently deleted and disassociated from the parent. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the parent asset.

parameters.name: relatedAssetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the related asset to delete.

200.description: The related asset was deleted successfully.

post.summary: Updates the transcription content for an asset.
post.description: Behavior: Replaces or updates specific segments of the transcription associated with the specified asset and transcript ID. Use this to correct auto-generated transcription text or adjust word timings. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The transcription data is updated and re-indexed. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset containing the transcription.

parameters.name: transcriptId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the transcription to update.

requestBody.description: The list of transcription updates to apply.

200.description: The transcription was updated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

get.summary: Records a video or ad tracking beacon event.
get.description: Behavior: Records a playback tracking event (e.g., play, pause, quartile, complete) for analytics and reporting. Supports both asset-level and live channel tracking. Responds with an HTML "OK" suitable for use as a tracking pixel/beacon endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A tracking record is created for analytics purposes. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. No tracking is recorded if neither assetId nor livechannelId is provided.

parameters.name: trackingEvent
parameters.description: The type of tracking event (e.g., play, pause, complete).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The ID of the asset being tracked. Required if not tracking a live channel.

parameters.name: livechannelId
parameters.description: The ID of the live channel being tracked. Required if not tracking an asset.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: Optional content ID to associate with the tracking event.

parameters.name: second
parameters.description: The second mark into the video or ad when the event occurred.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Optional profile ID to associate the tracking data with a specific viewer.

200.description: The tracking event was recorded successfully.

head.summary: Records a video or ad tracking beacon event.
head.description: Behavior: Records a playback tracking event (e.g., play, pause, quartile, complete) for analytics and reporting. Supports both asset-level and live channel tracking. Responds with an HTML "OK" suitable for use as a tracking pixel/beacon endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A tracking record is created for analytics purposes. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. No tracking is recorded if neither assetId nor livechannelId is provided.

parameters.name: trackingEvent
parameters.description: The type of tracking event (e.g., play, pause, complete).

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The ID of the asset being tracked. Required if not tracking a live channel.

parameters.name: livechannelId
parameters.description: The ID of the live channel being tracked. Required if not tracking an asset.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: Optional content ID to associate with the tracking event.

parameters.name: second
parameters.description: The second mark into the video or ad when the event occurred.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Optional profile ID to associate the tracking data with a specific viewer.

200.description: The tracking event was recorded successfully.

post.summary: Creates a downloadable ZIP archive containing multiple assets.
post.description: Behavior: Packages the specified assets into a single ZIP archive for download. You can optionally specify a custom filename and a download proxy URL. The request must include at least one asset ID. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A ZIP archive is created asynchronously. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing or contains no asset IDs. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the request model is null or invalid.

requestBody.description: The download request containing asset IDs, optional filename, and download proxy settings.

200.description: The archive creation was initiated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or contains no asset IDs.

404.description: The request model is null or invalid.

patch.summary: Updates specific properties of an asset such as its display name or custom metadata.
patch.description: Behavior: Updates the specified properties on the asset without affecting other attributes. Use this for lightweight property changes. For more complex operations (e.g., clipping, language change, archiving), use the dedicated endpoints. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The asset's search index entry is updated with the new properties. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to update.

requestBody.description: The properties to update on the asset.

200.description: The asset properties were updated successfully.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Associates a detected face in an asset with a known person.
post.description: Behavior: Links the specified detected face (identified by its external face ID) to a person record. This is used to confirm or assign facial recognition matches, updating the face's match status from probable to confirmed. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The face record is updated with the person association. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset containing the detected face.

parameters.name: faceId
parameters.description: The external face identifier from the facial recognition analysis.

parameters.name: personId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the person to associate with the face.

200.description: The person was assigned to the face successfully.

post.summary: Removes the person association from a detected face in an asset.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the link between a detected face and its assigned person. The face detection data is retained, but it will no longer be associated with a specific person record. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The face record's person association is removed. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset containing the detected face.

parameters.name: faceId
parameters.description: The external face identifier to remove the person association from.

200.description: The person association was removed successfully.

post.summary: Adds assets to the download queue for batch downloading.
post.description: Behavior: Adds the specified assets to the user's download queue. Queued assets can be processed for bulk download at a later time. This is useful for collecting multiple assets before initiating a single download operation. Folder and Collection Support: If a folder or collection ID is provided, all file assets within that folder or collection are automatically added to the queue. Only file assets are queued; folder and collection IDs themselves are never added. Threshold Logic: If the request contains 50 or fewer asset IDs, they are processed synchronously. If more than 50 asset IDs are provided, a background task is queued instead. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: New entries are created in the download queue, or a background task is queued. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if no asset IDs are provided. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The assets to add to the download queue.

200.description: The items were added to the download queue or queued for background processing.

400.description: No asset IDs were provided.

patch.summary: Updates the properties of a download queue item.
patch.description: Behavior: Updates an existing entry in the download queue, such as changing its priority or status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The download queue entry is updated. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The updated download queue item properties.

200.description: The queue item was updated.

delete.summary: Removes entries from the download queue.
delete.description: Behavior: Deletes the specified entries from the download queue. The entries are identified by their unique download queue IDs. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The specified queue entries are permanently removed. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The list of download queue entry IDs to delete.

200.description: The queue entries were deleted.

post.summary: Initiates a new multipart upload for a single asset.
post.description: Behavior: Prepares a new asset upload by creating the upload record and generating pre-signed URLs for each upload part. The client should upload each part directly to the provided URLs, then call the complete endpoint when all parts are uploaded. The file is split into parts based on the total file size. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: An upload record is created. Pre-signed upload URLs are generated with limited validity. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing or invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The upload parameters including file name, size, parent folder, and MIME type.

200.description: The upload was initiated and part URLs were generated.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Initiates a new multipart upload for a related asset (e.g., subtitle, proxy, thumbnail).
post.description: Behavior: Prepares an upload for a file that will be associated with an existing parent asset as a related asset. Generates pre-signed URLs for each upload part. Use this for uploading supplementary files such as subtitle tracks, proxy videos, or custom thumbnails. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: An upload record is created. Pre-signed upload URLs are generated. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is missing or invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The upload parameters including parent asset ID, file name, size, and relationship type.

200.description: The related asset upload was initiated and part URLs were generated.

400.description: The request body is missing or invalid.

post.summary: Completes a multipart upload after all parts have been transferred.
post.description: Behavior: Finalizes the multipart upload by combining all uploaded parts into the final asset file. This triggers post-upload processing including media analysis, transcoding, AI detection, and search indexing. Call this only after all parts have been uploaded and confirmed via the complete-part endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The uploaded parts are assembled into the final file. Asynchronous processing jobs (transcoding, AI analysis, indexing) are triggered. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the asset upload to complete.

200.description: The upload was completed and processing was initiated.

post.summary: Cancels an in-progress upload and deletes all uploaded parts.
post.description: Behavior: Aborts the multipart upload and cleans up all partially uploaded data. An optional message can be provided to record the reason for cancellation. Use this to abandon an upload that will not be completed. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: All uploaded parts are deleted from storage. The upload record is marked as cancelled. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the asset upload to cancel.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Optional reason for cancelling the upload.

200.description: The upload was cancelled and partial data was cleaned up.

post.summary: Confirms that an individual upload part was transferred successfully.
post.description: Behavior: Records that the specified upload part has been successfully uploaded to the pre-signed URL. The ETag returned by the storage service after uploading the part data must be provided to verify integrity. Call this for each part after uploading it, before calling the complete-upload endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The part is marked as completed in the upload record. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the ETag is missing or the request body is invalid. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of the upload part to mark as complete.

requestBody.description: The completion details including the ETag from the storage upload response.

200.description: The upload part was confirmed successfully.

400.description: The ETag is missing or the request body is invalid.

get.summary: Retrieves the audit history for a content item.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the chronological list of audit events recorded for the specified content item. Audit events include actions such as creation, updates, security changes, and other modifications performed on the content item. Authorization: No authorization required (controller does not have [Authorize] attribute). Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The content item identifier to retrieve audit history for.

200.description: The audit history was retrieved successfully.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Receives a Bitmovin encoding webhook callback.
post.description: Behavior: Processes a webhook callback from the Bitmovin encoding service. Packages the status, asset ID, encoding ID, and model data into a batch action message for asynchronous processing via the BitmovinUnitOfWork. Authorization: No authorization required. Validated by webhook filter. Side Effects: A batch action message is sent to trigger asynchronous processing of the encoding result. Error Scenarios: Returns 200 OK even on errors to acknowledge receipt. Errors are logged but swallowed to prevent webhook retry storms. Returns 200 OK with no processing if the model is null.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the encoding callback (e.g., finished, error).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset being encoded.

parameters.name: eId
parameters.description: The Bitmovin encoding identifier.

requestBody.description: The webhook payload containing encoding details.

200.description: Webhook callback was received and acknowledged.

post.summary: Imports Brightcove videos by their IDs.
post.description: Behavior: Imports the specified Brightcove videos into the system. When background mode is enabled (default), a batch action message is queued for asynchronous processing. When background mode is disabled, videos are archived synchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: In background mode, a batch action message is created and sent for asynchronous import. In foreground mode, videos are archived directly. Error Scenarios: Returns 204 No Content if the list of Brightcove IDs is null or empty. Returns 401 Unauthorized if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Whether to process the import asynchronously in the background. Defaults to true.

requestBody.description: A list of Brightcove video IDs to import.

200.description: Videos were imported or queued for import successfully.

204.description: No Brightcove IDs were provided.

post.summary: Imports Brightcove master videos with pagination support.
post.description: Behavior: Imports Brightcove master videos starting from the specified page number. When background mode is enabled (default), a batch action message is queued for asynchronous processing. When background mode is disabled, masters are imported synchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: In background mode, a batch action message is created and sent for asynchronous import. In foreground mode, master videos are imported directly. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 Unauthorized if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: pageNumber
parameters.description: The page number to start importing from. Defaults to 0 if not specified.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Whether to process the import asynchronously in the background. Defaults to true.

200.description: Master videos were imported or queued for import successfully.

post.summary: Initiates a Canto asset import, either as a background batch job or synchronously.
post.description: Behavior: When background is true (default), queues a Nomad.Common.Enums.BatchActions.CantoImport batch action that the LargeAction Lambda will process asynchronously. When false, runs the archive import synchronously for the requested page. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: Background mode sends a message to the batch action queue. Foreground mode archives the specified page of Canto assets directly. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: When true, the import is queued as a background batch job. When false, it runs synchronously. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: startingPageNumber
parameters.description: The zero-based page number to begin importing from. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of assets to process per page. Defaults to 20.

200.description: The import was successfully queued (background) or completed (foreground).

post.summary: Initiates a Canto asset import for a specific list of Canto IDs, either as a background batch job or synchronously.
post.description: Behavior: When background is true (default), queues a Nomad.Common.Enums.BatchActions.CantoImport batch action for the specified Canto IDs. When false, archives the specified assets directly. If the list is null or empty, returns 204 No Content. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: Background mode sends a message to the batch action queue. Foreground mode archives the specified Canto assets directly. Error Scenarios: Returns 204 if cantoIds is null or empty. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: When true, the import is queued as a background batch job. When false, it runs synchronously. Defaults to true.

requestBody.description: A list of Canto asset identifiers to import.

200.description: The import was successfully queued (background) or completed (foreground).

204.description: The provided Canto ID list was null or empty — no action taken.

get.summary: Retrieves the list of counties and their associated live streams.
get.description: Behavior: Performs two search index queries: one to retrieve all county records and another to retrieve all enabled live streams. Counties are returned as lookup models with their IDs and descriptions. Live streams are returned as asset reference models enriched with description, short description, associated county IDs, live channel status, status name, and output video URL. Disabled streams are excluded from results. Authorization: No authorization required. This is a publicly accessible endpoint. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: If no counties or streams are found, the response returns an empty Nomad.Api.Portal.CoJudicial.Models.StreamsModel with empty county and stream lists. If the search service encounters an error, the exception propagates to the caller.

200.description: Returns the streams model containing counties and live streams.

400.description: The request was invalid.

get.summary: Gets a specific collection by its unique identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Retrieves a single collection record matching the specified ID. Returns the full collection model if found. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is malformed. Returns 404 if no collection exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to retrieve.

200.description: The collection was found and returned successfully.

400.description: The request is malformed.

404.description: No collection exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific collection by its unique identifier.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the collection with the specified ID and returns the ID of the deleted collection. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Permanently removes the collection record from the database. Content items previously associated with this collection will lose that association. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the deletion fails or the collection ID is invalid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the collection to delete.

200.description: The collection was successfully deleted.

400.description: The deletion failed or the collection ID is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Removes collection associations from the specified content items.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the collection-to-content associations for each content item in the provided list. Note that this does NOT delete the collections themselves, only the associations between collections and content items. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Removes metadata associations between content items and collections. The collections and content items themselves remain intact. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of collection-content associations to remove, containing the record and collection identifiers.

200.description: The collection-content associations were successfully removed.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

404.description: The items list is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Creates a new collection or updates an existing one.
post.description: Behavior: If the input model has no ID, creates a new collection with the provided name. If an ID is present, updates the existing collection. This design intentionally avoids the need for a separate PUT operation. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates a new collection record in the database or updates an existing one. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the name is null.

requestBody.description: The collection model containing the name and optional ID. If ID is null, a new collection is created; otherwise the existing collection is updated.

200.description: The collection was successfully created or updated.

400.description: The input is null or the collection name is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Adds collection associations to the specified content items.
post.description: Behavior: Creates collection-to-content associations for each content item in the provided list. If a referenced collection does not exist, the createNew flag in the model determines whether the collection should be automatically created. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates metadata associations between content items and collections. May also create new collection records if the createNew flag is set. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of collection-content associations to create.

200.description: The collection-content associations were successfully created.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

404.description: The items list is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Retrieves all comments associated with a specific content item.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a list of all comments for the specified content ID. If the content ID has no comments, an empty list is returned. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the contentId is not a valid GUID. Returns 404 if the content item is not found.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content item to retrieve comments for.

200.description: The comments were successfully retrieved.

400.description: The request was invalid (e.g., malformed content ID).

404.description: The content item was not found.

post.summary: Creates a new comment on a specific content item.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new comment record associated with the specified content ID. The contentId from the route is assigned to the input model before saving. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: A new comment record is persisted in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid. Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content item to create the comment for.

requestBody.description: The comment model containing the comment details to create.

200.description: The comment was successfully created.

400.description: The request body was null or invalid.

put.summary: Updates an existing comment.
put.description: Behavior: Updates the comment identified by the specified comment ID with the provided model data. The commentId from the route is assigned to the input model before saving. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: The existing comment record is updated in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid. Returns 404 if the comment with the specified ID does not exist.

parameters.name: commentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment to update.

requestBody.description: The comment model containing the updated comment details.

200.description: The comment was successfully updated.

400.description: The request body was null or invalid.

404.description: The comment with the specified ID was not found.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific comment by its identifier.
delete.description: Behavior: Verifies the comment exists, then permanently deletes it from the data store. Returns the ID of the deleted comment. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: The comment record is permanently removed from the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if the comment with the specified ID does not exist. Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated.

parameters.name: commentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the comment to delete.

200.description: The comment was successfully deleted.

400.description: The request was invalid.

404.description: The comment with the specified ID was not found.

get.summary: Retrieves the current UTC server time.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the current server time in UTC. This endpoint is useful for client-server time synchronization. Authorization: No authorization required. This is a public endpoint. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: None expected under normal operation.

200.description: Returns the current server time in UTC.

get.summary: Retrieves the system configuration model for the specified configuration type.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the configuration model matching the requested type. For `Admin`, returns the full admin configuration model. For `Lambda`, currently returns an empty OK response (Lambda config is commented out). Authorization: Authorization is currently disabled (TODO) pending legacy admin app updates. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Throws an exception if the specified config type is not handled by any switch case.

parameters.name: configType
parameters.description: The type of configuration to retrieve. Defaults to `Admin`.

200.description: Returns the configuration model for the specified type.

500.description: The specified config type is not handled.

post.summary: Clears all server-side caches and invalidates the CloudFront Public API distribution.
post.description: Behavior: Removes all entries from the cache repository, reinitializes the configuration service, invalidates the CloudFront Public API distribution with a wildcard path, and clears the in-memory ListManager. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user with admin privileges. Returns an error if the user is not an admin. Side Effects: Clears all cached data across the system, invalidates the CloudFront CDN distribution, and reinitializes the configuration service. This operation affects all users of the system. Error Scenarios: Throws a VibrantException if the current user does not have admin permissions.

200.description: Server cache was successfully cleared and CloudFront distribution invalidated.

403.description: The user does not have admin permissions.

post.summary: Adds tag associations to content items in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Associates the specified tags with each content item in the list. If a tag does not already exist and the createNew flag is set on the model, the tag is created automatically. Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates tag-to-content associations. May create new tags if the createNew flag is set. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of tag-content associations to create.

200.description: The tag associations were processed successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Removes tag associations from content items in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the specified tag associations from each content item in the list. This does not delete the tags themselves, only the associations between tags and content items. Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Deletes tag-to-content association records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of tag-content associations to remove.

200.description: The tag associations were removed successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Creates related content associations in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Creates "related content" links between content items as specified in the list. Related content represents semantic relationships between items (e.g., "see also", "part of series"). Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates related content association records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of related content associations to create.

200.description: The related content associations were created successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Removes related content associations in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the specified "related content" links between content items. This does not delete the content items themselves, only the relationship associations. Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Deletes related content association records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of related content associations to remove.

200.description: The related content associations were removed successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Adds collection associations to content items in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Associates the specified collections with each content item in the list. If a collection does not already exist and the createNew flag is set on the model, the collection is created automatically. Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates collection-to-content associations. May create new collections if the createNew flag is set. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of collection-content associations to create.

200.description: The collection associations were processed successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Removes collection associations from content items in bulk.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the specified collection associations from each content item in the list. This does not delete the collections themselves, only the associations between collections and content items. Returns a batch result with per-item status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Deletes collection-to-content association records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of collection-content associations to remove.

200.description: The collection associations were removed successfully.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

post.summary: Executes a batch action on content items by action name.
post.description: Behavior: Performs the specified batch action on the content items identified in the request body. The action name is provided in the route and is matched (case-insensitive) against the BatchActions enum. Common actions include Delete, Archive, Restore, Duplicate, Move, Copy, Index, Reprocess, AddTag, RemoveTag, AddCollection, and RemoveCollection. The "Asset Metadata" content definition is reserved and cannot be targeted by batch actions. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Modifies the targeted content items according to the specified action. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the action name is not a valid BatchActions value, the request body is null, or the content definition is the reserved Asset Metadata definition.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The batch action to perform (case-insensitive, e.g., "delete", "archive", "addTag").

requestBody.description: The batch model containing target content IDs and action arguments.

200.description: The batch action completed and results are returned.

400.description: The action name is invalid, the body is null, or the content definition is reserved.

post.summary: Performs bulk metadata updates (collections, tags, related content, custom metadata) across multiple content items.
post.description: Behavior: Applies the specified property changes to all targeted content items. If the number of content items exceeds the configured MaxBulkMetadataInlineUpdateSize threshold, or if the background flag is set, the operation is queued as a batch action for asynchronous processing. Otherwise, the updates are applied inline. The "Asset Metadata" content definition is reserved and cannot be targeted. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Modifies metadata on all targeted content items. When processed in background mode, creates a batch job for asynchronous execution. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the content definition is reserved, content IDs are missing, or property changes are missing.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: When true, forces background batch processing regardless of item count. Defaults to false.

requestBody.description: The payload containing the content IDs and property changes to apply.

200.description: The bulk metadata update completed or was queued successfully.

400.description: The content definition is reserved, content IDs are empty, or property changes are empty.

get.summary: Retrieves all content records for a given content definition with pagination and sorting support.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a paginated, sorted list of content records belonging to the specified content definition. Excludes asset metadata and asset content definitions, which must be accessed through the Asset endpoint. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have permission to the specified content definition. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition ID is invalid, if the user lacks permission, or if the result is null.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition to retrieve content for.

parameters.name: sortColumn
parameters.description: The column name to sort by. Defaults to the system default sort column.

parameters.name: isDesc
parameters.description: Whether to sort in descending order. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: The zero-based page index. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of records per page. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional language filter GUID for multilingual content.

200.description: Returns the paginated list of content records.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid, reserved, or the user lacks permission.

get.summary: Finds content records within a content definition by matching a specific property value, with pagination and sorting.
get.description: Behavior: Searches for content records in the specified content definition where the given property name matches the provided value. Results are paginated and sorted according to the supplied parameters. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have permission to the specified content definition. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, if the user lacks permission, or if the search result is null.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition to search within.

parameters.name: propertyName
parameters.description: The name of the property to match against.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: The value to search for in the specified property.

parameters.name: sortColumn
parameters.description: The column name to sort by. Defaults to the system default sort column.

parameters.name: isDesc
parameters.description: Whether to sort in descending order. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: The zero-based page index. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of records per page. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Optional language filter GUID for multilingual content.

200.description: Returns the matching content records.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid, reserved, or the user lacks permission.

get.summary: Retrieves a single content record by its ID within a given content definition.
get.description: Behavior: Loads the content record matching the specified ID from the given content definition. Can optionally load a revision instead of the current version. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have permission to the specified content definition. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, or if the user lacks permission. Returns 404 Not Found if the content record does not exist.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to retrieve.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition the record belongs to.

parameters.description: If true, retrieves the revision version of the content record. Defaults to false.

200.description: Returns the requested content record.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid, reserved, or the user lacks permission.

404.description: The content record was not found.

post.summary: Creates a new content record within a content definition.
post.description: Behavior: Persists a new content record using the provided content update model. On success, returns a 201 Created response with a location header pointing to the newly created content record. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have write or higher permission to the content definition. Read-only and Guest permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: Creates a new content record in the database. May trigger notifications depending on content definition configuration. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, if the user lacks sufficient write permissions, or if the save operation fails.

requestBody.description: The content update model containing the data for the new content record.

201.description: The content record was successfully created.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid, reserved, the user lacks write permission, or save failed.

put.summary: Updates an existing content record within a content definition.
put.description: Behavior: Replaces the content record data with the provided content update model. On success, returns a 201 Created response with a location header pointing to the updated content record. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have write or higher permission to the content definition. Read-only and Guest permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: Updates the content record in the database. May trigger notifications depending on content definition configuration. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, if the user lacks sufficient write permissions, or if the save operation fails.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to update.

requestBody.description: The content update model containing the updated data.

201.description: The content record was successfully updated.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid, reserved, the user lacks write permission, or save failed.

delete.summary: Deletes a content record from the specified content definition.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the specified content record from the given content definition. This operation cannot be undone. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have delete-level permission to the content definition. Read, Guest, and FileWrite permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: Permanently removes the content record from the database. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, or if the user lacks sufficient delete permissions. Returns 404 Not Found if the content record does not exist or could not be deleted.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to delete.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition the record belongs to.

404.description: The content record was not found or could not be deleted.

get.summary: Retrieves the revision history list for a specific content record.

Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have permission to the specified content definition. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata, if the content definition is invalid, if the user lacks permission, or if the history list is null.

parameters.name: id

Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have write or higher permission to the content definition. Read-only and Guest permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: None. The content is not saved until a subsequent POST/PUT call. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the content definition is invalid, or if the user lacks sufficient write permissions.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition to create a new content template for.

post.summary: Duplicates an existing content record within a content definition, creating a new in-memory copy with fresh identifiers.
post.description: Behavior: Loads the specified content record and creates a duplicate copy with a new ID, new MasterId, fresh timestamps, and cleared modification user fields. The duplicate is returned but not yet persisted; the client must submit it via a create/save endpoint to persist the copy. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have write or higher permission to the content definition. Read-only and Guest permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: None. The duplicated content is not persisted until a subsequent save call. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata or assets, if the original content record does not exist, if the content definition is invalid, or if the user lacks sufficient write permissions.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to duplicate.

400.description: The content record or definition is invalid, reserved, or the user lacks write permission.

patch.summary: Partially updates specific properties of an existing content record using a property dictionary.
patch.description: Behavior: Merges the supplied properties into the existing content record. Only fields defined in the content definition's field list are updated; hard-coded system properties (e.g., security, timestamps) are skipped. If no changes are detected, the save operation is skipped but the endpoint still returns success. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must have write or higher permission to the content definition. Read-only and Guest permissions are insufficient. Side Effects: Updates the specified properties in the content record. Notifications can be suppressed via the query parameter. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID refers to asset metadata, if required IDs are empty, if properties are null or empty, if the content definition is invalid, if the user lacks write permissions, or if the content record does not exist.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition containing the record.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to patch.

parameters.name: suppressNotifications
parameters.description: If true, suppresses any notifications that would normally be triggered by the update. Defaults to false.

requestBody.description: A case-insensitive dictionary of property names and their new values.

Note that we convert all incoming keys to lower first char to help with serialization for JSON later

201.description: The content record properties were successfully patched.

get.summary: Records or updates a user session touch event for tracking content viewing activity.
get.description: Behavior: Creates a new user session record or updates the last activity date on an existing session for the specified content record. Captures browser type, OS, device type, and user email. This endpoint is used as a heartbeat mechanism to track active content viewers. Authorization: Requires authentication with a valid user ID. Side Effects: Creates or updates a content user session record in the database. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the authenticated user does not have a valid user ID.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition containing the tracked content.

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record being tracked.

204.description: The user session was successfully created or updated.

400.description: The user does not have a valid user ID.

post.summary: Updates the deactivation status of a content user tracking session.
post.description: Behavior: Sets the deactivated flag on the specified user session record. This is typically used to mark a user as no longer actively viewing content or to reactivate a previously deactivated session. Authorization: Requires authentication. Side Effects: Updates the deactivation status of the user session record in the database. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the session ID does not correspond to an existing session.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition (used for routing).

parameters.name: contentId

get.summary: Retrieves a paginated list of user tracking sessions for a specific content record.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all user session records associated with the specified content record, including details such as browser type, email, IP address, session start date, and last activity date. Results are paginated and sorted. Authorization: Requires authentication. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: None expected under normal operation; returns an empty list if no sessions exist.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition (used for routing).

parameters.name: contentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content record to retrieve user sessions for.

parameters.name: sortColumn
parameters.description: The column name to sort by. Defaults to the system default sort column.

parameters.name: isDesc

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: The zero-based page index. Defaults to the system default.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of records per page. Defaults to the system default.

get.summary: Retrieves all content definitions accessible to the current user, with optional filtering by content management type.

Results are filtered by the user's security permissions. Authorization: Requires authentication. Results are filtered to only include definitions the user has permission to access. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: None expected under normal operation; returns an empty list if no accessible definitions exist.

get.summary: Retrieves a single content definition by its unique identifier.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition to update.

204.description: The content instance and its children were successfully deleted.

get.summary: Renders the HTML content for a specific content instance, including associated scripts and styles.
get.description: Behavior: Loads the parent page of the specified content instance (to include the full instance hierarchy), then renders the HTML content for the given content instance. Returns the rendered HTML along with associated scripts and style assets. Authorization: No explicit authorization required. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 Not Found if the parent page for the content instance cannot be found.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content instance to render.

200.description: Returns the rendered content instance with associated scripts and styles.

404.description: The parent page for the content instance was not found.

get.summary: Creates a new empty content instance model template for a given content definition, ready for configuration.
get.description: Behavior: Generates a new content instance entity with default properties from the content definition transformer, then converts it to a model. This does not persist the instance; it only creates the template structure. Authorization: No explicit authorization required. Side Effects: None. The instance is not saved until a subsequent POST call. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the content definition ID is invalid or the transformer could not be found.

parameters.name: contentDefinitionId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content definition to create a new instance for.

200.description: Returns the new content instance model template.

400.description: The content definition ID is invalid or the transformer was not found.

patch.summary: Updates the rank sequence, parent content instance, and drop zone for a batch of content instances on the same page.
patch.description: Behavior: Accepts a list of content instance location models and updates the position-related properties (parentContentInstanceId, dropZone, rankSeq) for each instance. All instances must belong to the same page. Also updates the page's last modified date. Authorization: No explicit authorization required. Side Effects: Updates the rank sequence and positioning of content instances in the database and updates the parent page's last modified date. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the items list is null or empty, or if the parent page cannot be found.

requestBody.description: A list of content instance location models containing the new positioning data.

204.description: The rank sequences were successfully updated.

400.description: The items list is null or empty, or the parent page was not found.

patch.summary: Updates a batch of content instances by applying changes to each one sequentially.
patch.description: Behavior: Iterates through the provided list of content instance models and applies updates to each one. Each instance is updated individually within its parent page. Authorization: No explicit authorization required. Side Effects: Updates multiple content instances and their parent pages' last modified dates in the database. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the items list is null or empty.

requestBody.description: A list of content instance models to update.

204.description: All content instances were successfully updated.

400.description: The items list is null or empty.

post.summary: Creates one or more content labels in a single batch operation.
post.description: Behavior: Accepts a list of label models and creates them in the system. The label IDs are updated in-place on the input models, so the returned list contains the same objects with their newly assigned IDs. Authorization: Requires authentication. Side Effects: Creates new label records in the database. The input list objects are mutated with new IDs. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the list is null or empty (contains zero labels).

requestBody.description: A list of label models to create. Must contain at least one label.

200.description: Returns the created labels with their assigned IDs.

400.description: The list is null or contains zero labels.

get.summary: Retrieves all content types in the system, ordered alphabetically by title.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all available content types converted to models and sorted by title. The result includes the total item count for client-side pagination awareness. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must be an admin user. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the user is not an admin.

200.description: Returns the list of all content types.

400.description: The user is not an admin.

get.summary: Retrieves an embedded iframe video player for a given asset.
get.description: Behavior: Generates an HTML iframe representation of the specified media asset. If the asset is not found or an error occurs during retrieval, an HTML error page is returned instead of throwing an exception. Authorization: No authorization required. This is a publicly accessible endpoint for embedded content. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: If the asset ID is empty or null, a validation exception is thrown. If the asset cannot be found, a not-found exception is thrown. Any other errors are caught and an HTML error page is returned with the error message.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset to embed.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the iframe player in pixels. Defaults to 711 if not specified.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the iframe player in pixels. Defaults to 400 if not specified.

parameters.name: autoplay
parameters.description: Whether the media should autoplay when loaded. Defaults to true.

200.description: Returns the HTML iframe content for the embedded media player.

404.description: The specified asset could not be found.

get.summary: Retrieves an embedded media model for a given asset, share, or live channel.
get.description: Behavior: Looks up the media by the provided ID, which can be a public asset ID, a share ID, or a live channel ID. If the media is found, returns a JSON model. If an error occurs, falls back to returning an HTML error page as a ContentResult. Authorization: No authorization required. This is a publicly accessible endpoint for embedded content. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: If the ID is empty or null, a validation exception is thrown. If the media cannot be found, a not-found exception is thrown. Any other errors result in an HTML error page being returned with content type text/html.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The public asset ID, share ID, or live channel ID to retrieve media for.

parameters.name: assetId
parameters.description: An optional asset ID within a share to retrieve a specific asset.

200.description: Returns the embedded media model as JSON, or an HTML error page on failure.

404.description: The specified media could not be found.

get.summary: Generates a server-side HTML page with social media metadata tags for sharing media.
get.description: Behavior: Produces a full HTML page with dynamically injected Open Graph and social media metadata tags suitable for link previews. The asset can be identified either by a direct asset ID or by a stream URL. When a stream URL is provided without an ID, the asset is looked up by its path in the content bucket. When an ID is provided, it is validated as a share and checked for expiration before rendering. Autoplay can be specified as "true", "false", "1", or "0". Authorization: No authorization required. This is a publicly accessible endpoint for shared and embedded content. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: If a share ID is provided but the share is unavailable or expired, an HTML error page is returned. If neither a valid ID nor a resolvable stream URL is provided, an HTML error page indicating the media or share could not be found is returned. Any unexpected errors during rendering also produce an HTML error page.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The asset ID or share ID. Optional if streamUrl is provided.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the embedded player in pixels. Defaults to 711 if not specified.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the embedded player in pixels. Defaults to 400 if not specified.

parameters.name: streamUrl
parameters.description: The full URL path to the asset stream. Must be URL-encoded. Optional if id is provided.

parameters.name: autoplay
parameters.description: Whether the media should autoplay. Accepts "true", "false", "1", or "0". Defaults to "true".

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: An optional title for the shared video, used in metadata tags.

200.description: Returns an HTML page with embedded media and social metadata tags, or an HTML error page.

404.description: The specified asset or share could not be found.

get.summary: Generates a full HTML embed page for a live channel, suitable for use in an iframe.
get.description: Behavior: Resolves the live channel by ID, retrieves its signed HLS stream URL, and returns a full HTML page with the Nomad embed player configured in live mode (no seek bar, live indicator active). This endpoint is the live-channel equivalent of `share-media` — the returned page can be used directly as the `src` of an `

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to embed.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width of the embedded player in pixels. Defaults to 711 if not specified.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height of the embedded player in pixels. Defaults to 400 if not specified.

parameters.name: autoplay
parameters.description: Whether the stream should autoplay when loaded. Defaults to true.

200.description: Returns an HTML page with the live channel player, or an HTML error page.

404.description: The specified live channel could not be found or its stream is unavailable.

get.summary: Retrieves the shared asset list view for an embedded share player.
get.description: Behavior: Validates the share exists and has not expired, then searches for all file-type assets belonging to the share. Results include preview URLs and, conditionally, download URLs (if the share has download permissions and contains fewer than 500 items). Results are sorted by path depth (shallowest first) and then alphabetically by display path. Share metadata (name, expiration, permissions, type, status) is included in the related items of the response. Authorization: No authorization required. Access is controlled through share validation (existence and expiration checks). Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: If the share is unavailable, expired, or does not exist, a VibrantException with a NotFound status code is thrown.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the share to retrieve assets for.

200.description: Returns the list of shared assets with metadata.

400.description: The request was invalid.

404.description: The share was not found or has expired.

get.summary: Retrieves all field definitions in the system, ordered alphabetically by title.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all available field definitions converted to models and sorted by title. The result includes the total item count for client-side pagination awareness. Authorization: Requires authentication and the user must be an admin user. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 Bad Request if the user is not an admin.

200.description: Returns the list of all field definitions.

400.description: The user is not an admin.

post.summary: Receives a Hybrik encoding webhook callback.
post.description: Behavior: Processes a webhook callback from the Hybrik encoding service. Packages the model data into a batch action message for asynchronous processing via the HybrikUnitOfWork. Authorization: No authorization required. Validated by webhook filter. Side Effects: A batch action message is sent to trigger asynchronous processing of the encoding result. Error Scenarios: Returns 200 OK even on errors to acknowledge receipt. Errors are logged but swallowed to prevent webhook retry storms. Returns 200 OK with no processing if the model is null.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the asset being encoded.

requestBody.description: The webhook payload containing encoding details.

200.description: Webhook callback was received and acknowledged.

get.summary: Retrieves a pre-filled template for creating a new live channel.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a pre-populated Nomad.Common.Models.Live.LiveChannelNewModel with default values that can be modified and submitted to the create endpoint. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if the template cannot be generated.

200.description: The template was retrieved successfully.

404.description: The template could not be generated.

post.summary: Creates a new live channel and requests provisioning of the underlying system resource.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new live channel record and initiates resource provisioning. The channel is not available immediately; it will have a status of Nomad.Common.Enums.LiveChannelStatuses.Creating until provisioning completes. Use the M:Nomad.Controller.Live.LiveChannelController.New endpoint to obtain a starter template. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates a live channel record and initiates cloud resource provisioning.

requestBody.description: The live channel creation model with configuration details.

200.description: The live channel was created successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves all live channels in the system.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the complete list of live channels regardless of their current status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None.

200.description: The live channels were retrieved successfully.

put.summary: Updates an existing live channel configuration.
put.description: Behavior: Applies the changes from the edit model to the live channel. Note that underlying cloud resources may not be updated immediately even though the channel record reflects the changes. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the live channel record and may trigger cloud resource updates. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the edit model is null.

requestBody.description: The edit model containing the updated channel configuration.

200.description: The live channel was updated successfully.

400.description: The edit model is null.

get.summary: Retrieves a specific live channel by its identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the full live channel model for the specified channel ID. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to retrieve.

200.description: The live channel was retrieved successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes a live channel and its associated cloud resource.
delete.description: Behavior: Deletes the live channel record and requests deprovisioning of the underlying cloud resource. The resource may not be deleted immediately, and the channel record may remain temporarily accessible. The operation will fail if other resources (such as schedule events) still reference this channel. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Deletes the live channel record and initiates cloud resource deprovisioning.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to delete.

200.description: The live channel was deleted successfully.

post.summary: Creates a clip from a live channel stream.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates a clip operation on the specified live channel using the provided clip parameters (e.g., start time, duration). The clip is processed asynchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Initiates a clip extraction from the live stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to clip from.

requestBody.description: The clip parameters specifying the segment to extract.

200.description: The clip request was accepted successfully.

post.summary: Creates a snapshot clip from a live channel stream.
post.description: Behavior: Captures a snapshot clip from the specified live channel at the current playback position. Returns clip details including the generated asset information. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates a snapshot clip asset from the live stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to snapshot.

200.description: The snapshot clip was created successfully.

post.summary: Assembles a VOD HLS recording from Red5 partition-based HLS artifacts stored in S3.
post.description: Behavior: Scans the live channel's Red5 HLS upload location for partition folders that overlap the specified time window, aggregates the relevant video and subtitle segments, generates an assembled VOD HLS master manifest and variant playlists in S3, pre-registers a placeholder Nomad asset, and enqueues a batch action to materialize the recording. The live channel state is never modified by this operation. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Writes assembled manifest files to the channel's HLS S3 bucket and registers a placeholder asset. Does not modify the live channel entity. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the channel is not a Red5 HLS channel or no segments are found for the specified time range.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel.

requestBody.description: The recording request parameters.

200.description: The recording was assembled successfully.

400.description: The channel is not a Red5 HLS channel or no segments were found.

get.summary: Retrieves signed cookies or tokens for secure live stream playback.
get.description: Behavior: Generates and sets signed cookies on the response for authenticated access to the live stream CDN. If the stream uses token-based authentication instead, returns the playback token. This enables third-party integrations to obtain secured stream URLs per user. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Sets signed cookies on the HTTP response for CDN authentication. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if the channel has no configured output stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel.

200.description: The cookies or tokens were generated successfully.

404.description: The channel has no configured output stream.

put.summary: Updates the custom properties map for a live channel.
put.description: Behavior: Replaces or merges the specified key-value pairs into the live channel's properties dictionary. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the channel's custom properties in the data store.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel.

requestBody.description: A dictionary of property key-value pairs to update.

200.description: The properties were updated successfully.

post.summary: Starts output tracking for a live channel.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates output tracking for the specified live channel, enabling monitoring of the channel's output streams and viewer metrics. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Enables output tracking for the channel.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel.

200.description: Output tracking was started successfully.

post.summary: Starts a live channel.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a start command to the live channel, beginning the live stream. The channel must be in a valid state (e.g., idle or stopped) to be started. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Starts the live channel and begins streaming.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to start.

200.description: The channel was started successfully.

post.summary: Pauses a live channel.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a pause command to the live channel, temporarily suspending the live stream. The channel can be resumed later without restarting. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Pauses the live channel stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to pause.

200.description: The channel was paused successfully.

post.summary: Resumes a paused live channel.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a resume command to a previously paused live channel, continuing the live stream from where it was paused. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Resumes the live channel stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to resume.

200.description: The channel was resumed successfully.

post.summary: Stops a live channel.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a stop command to the live channel, ending the live stream. The channel can be started again later. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Stops the live channel stream.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to stop.

200.description: The channel was stopped successfully.

post.summary: Advances a live channel to its next scheduled event.
post.description: Behavior: Triggers a transition to the next event in the channel's schedule, skipping any remaining time on the current event. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Advances the channel schedule to the next event.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel.

200.description: The channel advanced to the next event successfully.

post.summary: Refreshes all live channels in the system.
post.description: Behavior: Synchronizes the state of all live channels with their underlying cloud resources, updating status and configuration information. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the cached state of all live channels.

200.description: All channels were refreshed successfully.

post.summary: Refreshes a specific live channel at a given point in time.
post.description: Behavior: Synchronizes the state of the specified live channel with its underlying cloud resource. The UTC date-time ticks parameter allows specifying the reference point for the refresh; if the value cannot be parsed, the current UTC time is used. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the cached state of the specified live channel.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the live channel to refresh.

parameters.name: utcDateTimeTicks
parameters.description: The UTC date-time ticks value for the refresh reference point.

200.description: The channel was refreshed successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves a pre-filled template for creating a new schedule event on a live channel.

post.summary: Creates a new schedule event on a live channel.

Side Effects: Creates a new schedule event and may affect the channel's playback schedule. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the channel ID in the body does not match the route. Returns 404 if the new schedule event model is null.

parameters.name: channelId

get.summary: Retrieves all schedule events for a live channel.

parameters.name: channelId

Requires an authenticated user with a valid bearer token.

until provisioning completes.

**Authorization:**

Cancels the broadcast session identified by the specified live operator identifier.

**Authorization:**

**Side Effects:**
- The broadcast session is terminated.
- The live operator record is updated to reflect cancellation.
            
**Error Scenarios:**
- Returns 401 if the request is not authenticated.

parameters.description: The unique identifier of the broadcast to cancel.

post.summary: Stops a live broadcast.
post.description:
**Behavior:**
Stops the broadcast session identified by the specified live operator identifier.
