200.description: The schedule was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No schedule found with the specified ID.

put.summary: Updates an existing schedule.
put.description: Behavior: Updates the schedule with the specified ID using the provided request data and returns the updated schedule model. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule record is updated in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to update.

requestBody.description: The schedule update request containing the new name and properties.

200.description: The schedule was updated successfully.

400.description: The request body was null.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific schedule.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the schedule with the specified ID and returns the deleted identifier. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule and its associated items are permanently removed from the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to delete.

200.description: The schedule was deleted successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves a preview of the schedule guide for a specific schedule.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a list of schedule guide items that represent the preview of what the schedule will look like when published. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to preview.

200.description: The schedule preview was retrieved successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves a specific schedule item by its identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the schedule item model for the specified item identifier. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no schedule item exists with the specified ID. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule item to retrieve.

200.description: The schedule item was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No schedule item found with the specified ID.

put.summary: Updates an existing schedule item.
put.description: Behavior: Updates the schedule item with the specified ID using the provided request data and returns the updated schedule item model. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule item record is updated in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The parent schedule ID.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule item to update.

requestBody.description: The schedule item update request.

200.description: The schedule item was updated successfully.

400.description: The request body was null.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific schedule item.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the schedule item with the specified ID and returns a change model reflecting the deletion and any cascading changes to the schedule. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule item is permanently removed and the schedule's item ordering may be updated. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The parent schedule ID.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule item to delete.

200.description: The schedule item was deleted successfully.

get.summary: Retrieves all schedule items for a specific schedule.
get.description: Behavior: Returns all schedule items belonging to the specified schedule, queried by the schedule's key. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule whose items to retrieve.

200.description: The schedule items were retrieved successfully.

post.summary: Creates a new schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new schedule with the specified name and returns the created schedule model. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new schedule record is persisted. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body or schedule name is null or empty. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

requestBody.description: The schedule creation request containing the name and optional ID.

200.description: The schedule was created successfully.

400.description: The request body or schedule name was null or empty.

post.summary: Creates a new schedule item within a schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new schedule item and associates it with the specified schedule. Returns a change model reflecting the created item and any cascading changes. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: A new schedule item record is persisted and the schedule's item ordering may be updated. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body or schedule ID is null or empty. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to add the item to.

requestBody.description: The schedule item creation request.

200.description: The schedule item was created successfully.

400.description: The request body or schedule ID was null or empty.

post.summary: Moves a schedule item to a new position within the schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Repositions the specified schedule item according to the move request and returns the updated list of all schedule items reflecting the new ordering. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule item ordering is updated in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The parent schedule ID.

parameters.name: itemId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule item to move.

requestBody.description: The move request specifying the new position.

200.description: The schedule item was moved successfully.

post.summary: Starts a schedule for live playback.
post.description: Behavior: Transitions the schedule to a Starting state and initiates channel playback. When background mode is enabled (default), a batch action message is queued for asynchronous processing. When background mode is disabled, the channel is started synchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule status is updated to Starting and a batch action message may be sent. The associated live channel begins playback. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the schedule is not found or is already in a Running, Starting, or Stopping state. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to start.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Whether to run the operation asynchronously in the background. Defaults to true.

200.description: The schedule start was initiated successfully.

400.description: The schedule was not found or is already running.

post.summary: Stops a running schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Transitions the schedule to a Stopping state and halts channel playback. When background mode is enabled (default), a batch action message is queued for asynchronous processing. When background mode is disabled, the channel is stopped synchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule status is updated to Stopping and a batch action message may be sent. The associated live channel stops playback. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the schedule is not found or is already in a Stopped state. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to stop.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Whether to run the operation asynchronously in the background. Defaults to true.

200.description: The schedule stop was initiated successfully.

400.description: The schedule was not found or is already stopped.

post.summary: Publishes an intelligent schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Publishes the intelligent schedule, optionally locking it for a specified number of days. When background mode is enabled (default), a batch action message is queued for asynchronous processing. When background mode is disabled, the schedule is published synchronously. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: The schedule is published and its expiration date may be set. A batch action message may be sent for asynchronous processing. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request body is null. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the intelligent schedule to publish.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Whether to run the operation asynchronously in the background. Defaults to true.

requestBody.description: The publish request containing the number of locked days.

200.description: The schedule was published successfully.

400.description: The request body was null.

post.summary: Bulk imports schedule items into a schedule.
post.description: Behavior: Creates multiple schedule items from the provided import models and associates them with the specified schedule. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Multiple schedule item records are persisted in the data store. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input list is null. Returns 401 if the caller is not authenticated.

parameters.name: scheduleId
parameters.description: The ID of the schedule to import items into.

requestBody.description: The list of schedule items to import.

200.description: The schedule items were imported successfully.

400.description: The input list was null.

get.summary: Retrieves all search index field names available for mapping and filtering.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the complete list of field names defined in the search index schema. These field names can be used to configure search filters, sort fields, and returned field lists. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None.

200.description: The field names were retrieved successfully.

post.summary: Executes a search query against the search index.
post.description: Behavior: Accepts a full search model with filters, sort fields, pagination, and query text. Returns a paginated list of matching search results. Use the SearchModel to control which fields are returned, how results are sorted, and which filters to apply. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the search model is null or contains invalid filter criteria.

parameters.name: includeInternalFieldsInResults
parameters.description: When true, includes internal system fields in the search result output. Defaults to false.

requestBody.description: The search query model containing filters, sort fields, pagination, and query text.

200.description: The search completed successfully.

400.description: The search model is null or invalid.

get.summary: Executes a search query using query string parameters for simple search scenarios.
get.description: Behavior: Provides a GET-based search interface where common search parameters can be passed as query strings. Query string values override any corresponding values in the optional request body SearchModel. Filters are extracted from additional query string parameters beyond the named ones. Sort order accepts "descending", "desc", or "1" for descending sort. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: The number of results to return per page. Overrides the value in the body model.

parameters.name: pageOffset
parameters.description: The pagination offset for the result set. Overrides the value in the body model.

parameters.name: searchQuery
parameters.description: The free-text search query. Overrides the value in the body model.

parameters.name: sortField
parameters.description: The field name to sort results by.

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: The sort direction: ascending (default) or descending.

parameters.name: includeInternalFieldsInResults
parameters.description: When true, includes internal system fields in the search result output. Defaults to false.

requestBody.description: Optional SearchModel in the request body for advanced search configuration.

200.description: The search completed successfully.

400.description: The search parameters are invalid.

post.summary: Exports search results as an Excel spreadsheet or queues the export for background processing.
post.description: Behavior: Executes the search query and exports matching results as an XLSX file. If no sort fields are provided, results are sorted by masterId by default. When the background flag is set, the export is queued as a batch action and the caller receives a 204 response immediately; the exported file is delivered via email when ready. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: When running in background mode, creates a batch job for asynchronous processing. Error Scenarios: Returns 204 if no records match the search criteria. Returns 400 if the search model is null or invalid.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: When true, queues the export for background processing and returns immediately. Defaults to false.

requestBody.description: The search query model defining which results to export.

200.description: The export file was generated successfully.

204.description: No matching records found, or background export was queued.

400.description: The search model is null or invalid.

post.summary: Rebuilds the search index using the specified rebuild strategy.
post.description: Behavior: Triggers a full or partial rebuild of the search index depending on the type parameter. Supported types: `$ALL$` (rebuild from all sources), `$ALL_CLEAR$` (clear index then rebuild), `$MONGO$` (rebuild from MongoDB source only), `$DYNAMO$` (rebuild from DynamoDB source only). Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Triggers a potentially long-running reindex operation. During a $ALL_CLEAR$ rebuild, search results may be temporarily unavailable. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the type parameter is null.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The rebuild strategy: `$ALL$`, `$ALL_CLEAR$`, `$MONGO$`, or `$DYNAMO$`.

200.description: The reindex operation completed successfully.

400.description: The type parameter is null or invalid.

get.summary: Retrieves a single indexed document by its unique identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Fetches a specific document from the search index by ID. Returns the full search result representation of the document including all indexed fields. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no document with the given ID exists in the index.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the indexed document to retrieve.

parameters.name: includeInternalFieldsInResults
parameters.description: When true, includes internal system fields in the result. Defaults to false.

200.description: The document was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No document found with the specified ID.

post.summary: Updates the security configuration for a content item.
post.description: Behavior: Replaces the security settings for the content item identified by ID. Security settings control which groups and users have access to the content. The inherit security flag determines whether the item inherits permissions from its parent. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates security group and user assignments for the content item. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the security model is null.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the content item to update security for.

requestBody.description: The security configuration containing groups, users, and inheritance settings.

200.description: The security settings were updated successfully.

400.description: The security model is null.

get.summary: Retrieves all users registered in the identity provider.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the full list of users from the identity provider (Cognito). To retrieve all users including those not in the identity provider, use the Search API instead. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None.

200.description: The user list was retrieved successfully.

post.summary: Creates or updates a user account.
post.description: Behavior: Saves the user profile. If the user does not exist, a new account is created. If the user already exists, the profile is updated with the provided values. Returns the saved user profile. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates or updates a user account in the identity provider and system. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user model is null. Returns 404 if the save operation fails.

requestBody.description: The admin user model to create or update.

200.description: The user was saved successfully.

400.description: The user model is null.

404.description: The save operation did not return a valid user.

get.summary: Retrieves a user by their unique identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the full admin user profile for the specified ID, including security groups, system roles, and folder assignments. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no user exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to retrieve.

200.description: The user was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No user found with the specified ID.

delete.summary: Deletes a user by their unique identifier.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently removes the user account identified by ID from both the identity provider and the system. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes the user account. This operation cannot be undone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to delete.

200.description: The user was deleted successfully.

put.summary: Updates an existing user account by ID.
put.description: Behavior: Fully replaces the user profile for the specified ID with the provided model. The ID in the route is used for REST convention; the user identity is determined from the model body. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the user account in the identity provider and system. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user model is null. Returns 404 if the save operation fails.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to update.

requestBody.description: The admin user model with updated values.

200.description: The user was updated successfully.

400.description: The user model is null.

404.description: The save operation did not return a valid user.

get.summary: Retrieves a user by their email address.
get.description: Behavior: Looks up a user by email address and returns the full admin user profile. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the email is null or empty. Returns 404 if no user exists with the specified email.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The email address to look up.

200.description: The user was retrieved successfully.

400.description: The email is null or empty.

404.description: No user found with the specified email address.

post.summary: Creates the required database tables for the application.
post.description: Behavior: Ensures all required database tables exist by creating any that are missing. This is an idempotent operation and can be safely called multiple times. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates database tables that do not already exist. Existing tables are not modified. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the database setup fails due to connectivity or permission issues.

200.description: Database tables were successfully created or already exist.

400.description: An error occurred during the database setup process.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Generates the built-in content definitions JSON file.
post.description: Behavior: Generates a JSON representation of the built-in content definitions and returns it as a string. This can be used to bootstrap or inspect the default content definition configuration. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: May write the generated content definitions file to storage depending on the implementation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the content definitions cannot be generated.

200.description: Content definitions JSON was successfully generated.

400.description: An error occurred while generating content definitions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Generates the schema registry in YAML format.
get.description: Behavior: Generates a YAML representation of all content definition schemas indexed by schema name. This provides a human-readable format suitable for documentation or configuration management. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Content Type: Returns YAML content with the 'application/x-yaml' content type. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the schema registry cannot be generated or if the user lacks admin permissions.

200.description: Schema registry YAML was successfully generated.

400.description: An error occurred while generating the schema registry.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Generates the schema registry in JSON format.
get.description: Behavior: Generates a JSON representation of all content definition schemas indexed by schema name. This provides a machine-readable format suitable for programmatic consumption. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Content Type: Returns JSON content with the 'application/json' content type. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the schema registry cannot be generated or if the user lacks admin permissions.

200.description: Schema registry JSON was successfully generated.

400.description: An error occurred while generating the schema registry.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Creates the default saved searches for the environment.
post.description: Behavior: Ensures that all default saved searches exist in the system. This is an idempotent operation; existing saved searches are not duplicated or modified. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates default saved search records in the database if they do not already exist. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the saved search creation fails.

200.description: Default saved searches were successfully created or already exist.

400.description: An error occurred while creating default saved searches.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Creates the default live output profile groups for the environment.
post.description: Behavior: Ensures that all default live output profile groups exist in the system. This is an idempotent operation; existing profile groups are not duplicated or modified. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates default live output profile group records if they do not already exist. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the profile group creation fails.

200.description: Default live output profile groups were successfully created or already exist.

400.description: An error occurred while creating default live output profile groups.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Triggers the December 2023 upgrade batch process.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a batch action message to initiate the December 2023 upgrade process. The upgrade runs asynchronously via the batch processing system. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Queues an upgrade batch action message for asynchronous processing. This may modify data schemas and records as part of the upgrade. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the batch message cannot be sent.

200.description: The upgrade batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: An error occurred while queuing the upgrade batch action.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Triggers the February 2024 full update upgrade batch process.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a batch action message to initiate the February 2024 FullUpdate upgrade process. Supports a preview-only mode that simulates the upgrade without making changes. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: When previewOnly is false, queues an upgrade batch action that modifies data records. When previewOnly is true, only simulates the upgrade. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the batch message cannot be sent.

parameters.name: previewOnly
parameters.description: When true, the upgrade is simulated without making changes. Defaults to true.

200.description: The upgrade batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: An error occurred while queuing the upgrade batch action.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Triggers the August 2024 share migration upgrade batch process.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a batch action message to initiate the August 2024 MigrateShares upgrade process including asset re-indexing. Supports a preview-only mode that simulates the upgrade without making changes. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: When previewOnly is false, queues an upgrade batch action that migrates shares and re-indexes assets. When previewOnly is true, only simulates the upgrade. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the batch message cannot be sent.

parameters.name: previewOnly
parameters.description: When true, the upgrade is simulated without making changes. Defaults to true.

200.description: The upgrade batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: An error occurred while queuing the upgrade batch action.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Triggers the December 2024 security permissions migration and live output profile groups migration upgrade batch process.
post.description: Behavior: Sends two batch action messages: one to migrate security permissions with asset re-indexing, and another to migrate live output profile groups to S3. Both support a preview-only mode that simulates the upgrade without making changes. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: When previewOnly is false, queues two upgrade batch actions that migrate security permissions, re-index assets, and migrate live output profile groups to S3. When previewOnly is true, only simulates the upgrades. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if either batch message cannot be sent.

parameters.name: previewOnly
parameters.description: When true, the upgrade is simulated without making changes. Defaults to true.

200.description: Both upgrade batch actions were successfully queued.

400.description: An error occurred while queuing the upgrade batch actions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Retrieves a share by its identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the share model for the specified identifier. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no share exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the share to retrieve.

200.description: The share was retrieved successfully.

404.description: No share found with the specified ID.

put.summary: Updates an existing share.
put.description: Behavior: Updates the share identified by the route ID with the values from the update model. Invalidates the share cache after the update. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Updates the share record and invalidates the share cache. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the update model is null. Returns 404 if the share does not exist.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the share to update.

requestBody.description: The share update model with new values.

200.description: The share was updated successfully.

400.description: The share update model is null.

404.description: No share found with the specified ID.

delete.summary: Deletes a share.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the share identified by the specified ID and invalidates the share cache. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Deletes the share record and invalidates the share cache.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the share to delete.

200.description: The share was deleted successfully.

post.summary: Creates a new share.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new share record using the provided add model and returns the created share. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Creates a new share record. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the add model is null. Returns 404 if the share could not be created.

requestBody.description: The share creation model.

200.description: The share was created successfully.

400.description: The share add model is null.

404.description: The share could not be created.

post.summary: Creates a share notification.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a notification for the specified share, such as an email notification to intended recipients. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Sends a notification (e.g., email) to the share recipients. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the notification model is null.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the share to notify about.

requestBody.description: The notification configuration model.

200.description: The notification was sent successfully.

400.description: The notification model is null.

post.summary: Force-expires one or all shares.
post.description: Behavior: Forces the expiration of the specified share, or all shares if no ID is provided. Invalidates the share cache after expiration. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Marks the share(s) as expired and invalidates the share cache.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: Optional share ID. If null, expires all shares.

200.description: The share(s) were expired successfully.

post.summary: Authenticates a user using a token and identifier for stage access.
post.description: Behavior: Validates the provided authentication token and identifier against the stage system. The token is normally supplied by the calling system as the unique identifier of the experience, and the token is the temporary authentication credential for the user. Returns 404 if validation fails. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: A stage session may be created or updated for the authenticated user. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the token validation fails or the user cannot be found.

requestBody.description: The Nomad.Common.Models.Security.AuthTokenRequestModel containing the token and identifier for authentication.

200.description: The user was successfully authenticated.

400.description: The request was invalid.

404.description: The token validation failed or the user was not found.

get.summary: Sends a ping to indicate the client is still connected to the stage session.
get.description: Behavior: Pings the server to tell it that the client is still connected. The userSessionId normally comes from the Login or AuthToken command. Optionally updates the user's screen name. Returns 404 if the session is not found. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: The session's last activity timestamp is updated. The user's screen name may be updated if the sn parameter is provided. Error Scenarios: Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the session ID is not found or has expired.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The user session ID of the logged-in user, typically obtained from the Login or AuthToken command.

parameters.name: sn
parameters.description: The latest screen name of the user (optional).

200.description: The ping was successfully processed.

400.description: The request was invalid.

404.description: The session ID was not found or has expired.

post.summary: Starts a new stage session for the authenticated user.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates a new stage session using the provided authentication token and identifier. The token is normally supplied by the calling system as the unique identifier of the experience. Returns 404 if the session cannot be started. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: A new stage session is created for the authenticated user. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid. Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the session could not be started.

requestBody.description: The Nomad.Common.Models.Security.AuthTokenRequestModel containing the token and identifier for session creation.

200.description: The session was successfully started.

400.description: The request body was null or invalid.

404.description: The session could not be started or the user was not found.

post.summary: Verifies an existing stage session is still valid.
post.description: Behavior: Validates that the specified stage session is still active and valid using the provided authentication token and identifier. Returns 404 if the session is invalid or expired. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: The session validity is checked but the session state may be updated as part of verification. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid. Returns 401 if the user is not authenticated. Returns 404 if the session is invalid or has expired.

requestBody.description: The Nomad.Common.Models.Security.AuthTokenRequestModel containing the token and identifier for session verification.

200.description: The session was successfully verified.

400.description: The request body was null or invalid.

404.description: The session is invalid or has expired.

get.summary: Retrieves all statistics records.
get.description: Behavior: Returns a complete list of all statistics records in the system without any date filtering. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the statistics retrieval fails.

200.description: Statistics records retrieved successfully.

400.description: An error occurred while retrieving statistics.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Retrieves statistics records within a specified date range.
get.description: Behavior: Returns statistics records filtered by the specified date range (inclusive of fromDate and toDate). Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the date range is invalid or the statistics retrieval fails.

parameters.name: fromDate
parameters.description: The start date of the range to query statistics for.

parameters.name: toDate
parameters.description: The end date of the range to query statistics for.

200.description: Statistics records for the date range retrieved successfully.

400.description: The date range is invalid or an error occurred while retrieving statistics.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

get.summary: Gets statistics measurement config data.
get.description: Behavior: Retrieves the configuration for available statistics measurements including measurement types, available periods, and display options. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the configuration retrieval fails.

200.description: Statistics measurement configuration retrieved successfully.

400.description: An error occurred while retrieving the measurement configuration.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Lists statistics measurement data from search indexes over the specified period.
post.description: Behavior: Queries the search indexes for measurement data matching the specified period and measurement types in the request model, and returns the results as graph-ready data. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request model is invalid or the measurement retrieval fails.

requestBody.description: A model containing period and measurements to retrieve.

200.description: Statistics measurements retrieved successfully.

400.description: The request model is invalid or an error occurred while retrieving measurements.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Re-generates rollup snapshot data for the specified date and re-indexes it into the search index.
post.description: Behavior: Re-runs the Athena-backed rollup aggregation for the specified date (defaults to yesterday) and re-indexes the results into OpenSearch. Use this to rebuild the search index for a specific day without waiting for the nightly schedule. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Modifies the search index by indexing freshly computed rollup measurements. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the rollup regeneration fails.

parameters.name: snapshotDate
parameters.description: The date to regenerate. Defaults to yesterday if not specified.

200.description: Rollup was successfully regenerated and indexed.

400.description: An error occurred while regenerating the rollup.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

put.summary: Generates rollup snapshot data for the specified date and stores it in S3.
put.description: Behavior: Generates rollup measurement snapshots for the specified date (or the current date if not provided) and stores them in S3. These snapshots can later be restored using the POST measurements/rollup endpoint. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates or overwrites rollup snapshot files in S3 for the specified date. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the snapshot generation fails.

parameters.name: snapshotDate
parameters.description: The date of rollups snapshot to generate. If null, defaults to the current date.

200.description: Rollup snapshots were successfully generated.

400.description: An error occurred while generating rollup snapshots.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Exports statistics journal entries for the specified date range as an Excel file.
post.description: Behavior: Exports journal entries for the specified date range. When background is true (default), the export is queued as a batch action for asynchronous processing. When background is false, the export runs synchronously and returns the Excel file directly. If no data is available, returns 204 No Content. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: When background is true, queues a batch export job. When background is false, generates an Excel file in memory. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the export date is invalid or the export process fails. Returns 204 if no journal data exists for the specified date range.

parameters.name: exportDate
parameters.description: The start date for the journal export.

parameters.name: exportEndDate
parameters.description: The optional end date for the journal export. If null, only the export date is used.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: When true, the export runs as a background batch job. When false, the export runs synchronously and returns the file. Defaults to true.

200.description: The journal export was completed and the file is returned (synchronous mode only).

204.description: The export was queued as a background job or no data exists for the specified date range.

400.description: The export date is invalid or an error occurred during the export.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Generates the system statistics for the environment.
post.description: Behavior: Triggers generation of system-level statistics. When forceReload is true, statistics are regenerated even if they already exist for the current period. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Additionally, the user must be an admin (checked via IsAdminUser). Side Effects: Generates and persists system statistics records. When forceReload is true, existing statistics may be overwritten. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user is not an admin or the statistics generation fails. Throws a VibrantException if the user lacks admin permissions.

parameters.name: forceReload
parameters.description: When true, forces statistics to regenerate even if they already exist for the current period.

200.description: Statistics were successfully generated.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions or statistics generation failed.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Queues an analytics rollup batch action to run in the LargeAction Lambda.
post.description: Behavior: Queues an analytics rollup generation as a batch action that will be processed by the LargeAction Lambda. This uses server-side OpenSearch aggregations for efficient processing of large catalogs. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user with admin permissions via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Sends a message to the batch action queue to trigger rollup generation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user is not an admin or the batch action fails to queue.

parameters.name: snapshotDate
parameters.description: The date to generate rollups for. If null, defaults to yesterday.

200.description: The analytics rollup batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions or the batch action failed to queue.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Queues a CloudFront log parse batch action to run in the LargeAction Lambda.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a message to the batch action SQS queue to trigger Phase 1 of the CloudFront views pipeline. The LargeAction Lambda will read new CloudFront access log files, extract video view events, deduplicate them, and write pending batch files for import. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user with admin permissions. Side Effects: Sends a message to the batch action queue; does not process synchronously. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user is not an admin.

200.description: The log parse batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Queues a CloudFront view import batch action to run in the LargeAction Lambda.
post.description: Behavior: Sends a message to the batch action SQS queue to trigger Phase 2 of the CloudFront views pipeline. The LargeAction Lambda will read pending view batch files from S3 and atomically increment per-asset daily and total view counts in DynamoDB. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user with admin permissions. Side Effects: Sends a message to the batch action queue; does not process synchronously. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user is not an admin.

200.description: The view import batch action was successfully queued.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Invalidates the CDN distribution cache for the specified paths.
post.description: Behavior: Sends an invalidation request to the CDN distribution for the paths specified in the input model. This forces the CDN to fetch fresh content from the origin on subsequent requests. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Triggers a CDN cache invalidation which may temporarily increase origin load. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid.

requestBody.description: The InvalidateDistrubutionModel input containing the distribution paths to invalidate.

200.description: Cache invalidation completed successfully.

400.description: The input model is null or invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Rebuilds the search index records for shares.
post.description: Behavior: Rebuilds the search index for a specific share (if shareId is provided) or all shares. Expired shares can be optionally excluded from the rebuild. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Modifies search index records for the affected shares. May be a long-running operation when rebuilding all shares. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if an error occurs during the rebuild process.

parameters.name: shareId
parameters.description: Optional ShareId. If missing, all shares are rebuilt.

parameters.name: excludeExpiredShares
parameters.description: Whether to exclude expired shares from the rebuild. Defaults to true.

200.description: Share index records rebuilt successfully.

400.description: An error occurred during the rebuild process.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Dispatches an email using the provided email configuration.
post.description: Behavior: Processes and sends an email based on the provided EmailDispatcherModel configuration. Only admin users are permitted to dispatch emails. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the [Authorize] attribute. Additionally, the user must be an admin (checked via IsAdminUser). Side Effects: Sends an email to the recipients specified in the input model. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null or invalid. Throws a VibrantException if the current user is not an admin.

requestBody.description: The EmailDispatcherModel input containing email configuration such as recipients, subject, and body.

200.description: Email dispatched successfully.

400.description: The input model is null or the user lacks admin permissions.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Tests the current license by performing a license check against the license server.
post.description: Behavior: Validates the current license by querying the license server. When forceReload is true, the cached license is bypassed and a fresh query is made to the license server. Authorization: No authorization attribute is applied to this endpoint. Side Effects: When forceReload is true, refreshes the cached license data from the license server. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the license check fails or the license server is unreachable.

parameters.name: forceReload
parameters.description: When true, forces the license check to requery the license server instead of using cached data.

200.description: License check completed successfully and the license grant is returned.

400.description: The license check failed or the license server could not be reached.

post.summary: Triggers a batch migration of assets from one storage platform to another.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates a batch action to migrate assets between storage platforms using the provided bucket replacement mappings. The platform setup must be completed before calling this endpoint. This endpoint only triggers the migration; the actual processing occurs asynchronously. Authorization: No [Authorize] attribute, but the user must be an admin (checked via IsAdminUser). Side Effects: Queues a batch migration job that moves assets between storage buckets. This is a long-running asynchronous operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if replaceBuckets is null or empty, or if the user is not an admin. Throws a VibrantException for permission or validation failures.

requestBody.description: A list of key-value pairs mapping source bucket names to destination bucket names.

200.description: The migration batch job was successfully initiated.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions or replaceBuckets is null or empty.

post.summary: Aligns the asset catalog by triggering a batch catalog alignment process.
post.description: Behavior: Initiates a batch action to align the asset catalog based on the provided alignment model. If no model is provided, default alignment settings are used. The actual alignment processing occurs asynchronously. Authorization: No [Authorize] attribute, but the user must be an admin (checked via IsAdminUser). Side Effects: Queues a batch catalog alignment job that processes and reconciles catalog entries asynchronously. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the user is not an admin. Throws a VibrantException for permission failures.

requestBody.description: The AlignCatalogModel containing alignment configuration options. If null, default settings are used.

200.description: The catalog alignment batch job was successfully initiated.

400.description: The user lacks admin permissions.

get.summary: Gets a specific tag by its unique identifier.
get.description: Behavior: Retrieves a single tag record matching the specified ID. Returns the full tag model if found. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: None. This is a read-only operation. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is malformed. Returns 404 if no tag exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tag to retrieve.

200.description: The tag was found and returned successfully.

400.description: The request is malformed.

404.description: No tag exists with the specified ID.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

delete.summary: Deletes a specific tag by its unique identifier.
delete.description: Behavior: Permanently deletes the tag with the specified ID and returns the ID of the deleted tag. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Permanently removes the tag record from the database. Content items previously associated with this tag will lose that association. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the deletion fails or the tag ID is invalid.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the tag to delete.

200.description: The tag was successfully deleted.

400.description: The deletion failed or the tag ID is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Removes tag associations from the specified content items.
post.description: Behavior: Removes the tag-to-content associations for each content item in the provided list. Note that this does NOT delete the tags themselves, only the associations between tags and content items. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Removes metadata associations between content items and tags. The tags and content items themselves remain intact. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of tag-content associations to remove, containing the record and tag identifiers.

200.description: The tag-content associations were successfully removed.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

404.description: The items list is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Creates a new tag or updates an existing one.
post.description: Behavior: Creates a new tag or updates an existing tag based on the input model. The underlying implementation uses a create-or-update pattern. This design intentionally avoids the need for a separate PUT operation. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates a new tag record in the database or updates an existing one. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the tag name is null.

requestBody.description: The tag model containing the name and optional configuration for the tag.

200.description: The tag was successfully created or updated.

400.description: The input is null or the tag name is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Adds tag associations to the specified content items.
post.description: Behavior: Creates tag-to-content associations for each content item in the provided list. If a referenced tag does not exist, the createNew flag in the model determines whether the tag should be automatically created. Authorization: Requires an authenticated user via the class-level [Authorize] attribute. Side Effects: Creates metadata associations between content items and tags. May also create new tag records if the createNew flag is set. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input is null or the items list is empty. Returns 404 if the items list is null.

requestBody.description: The list of tag-content associations to create.

200.description: The tag-content associations were successfully created.

400.description: The input is null or the items list is empty.

404.description: The items list is null.

401.description: Unauthorized. The caller must provide a valid Bearer token.

post.summary: Receives a Twelve Labs video understanding webhook callback.
post.description: Behavior: Processes webhook callbacks from the Twelve Labs API by deserializing the payload to extract the task ID and status, then sending a batch action message for asynchronous processing. The request is validated by the ValidateTwelveLabsWebhookRequestFilter. Authorization: No authorization required. Validated by webhook filter. Side Effects: A batch action message is sent to trigger asynchronous processing of the Twelve Labs task result. Error Scenarios: Returns 200 OK even on errors to acknowledge receipt. Errors are logged but swallowed to prevent webhook retry storms.

requestBody.description: The webhook payload as a JSON object returned by the Twelve Labs API.

200.description: Webhook callback was received and acknowledged.

delete.summary: Permanently deletes a user account and all associated data.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes the user account and all related data from the system. This is a destructive operation that cannot be undone. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes the user and all associated records.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user to delete.

200.description: The user was deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes a user's data without removing the user account itself.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all user-generated data (video tracking, content attributes, saved searches, content groups, favorites, likes, etc.) while preserving the user account. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's data. Only administrators can execute this operation; non-admin requests are silently skipped. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token with administrator privileges. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all user data records.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's data.

200.description: The user data was deleted successfully, or the operation was skipped for non-admin callers.

post.summary: Deletes video tracking data for a user, asset, content item, or combination thereof.
post.description: Behavior: Removes video tracking records based on the provided filter criteria. Supports filtering by user ID, asset ID, and/or content ID, which can be combined: user ID deletes all tracking for that user, asset ID deletes all tracking for that asset, content ID deletes all tracking for that content item. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes matching video tracking records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null.

requestBody.description: The filter criteria specifying which video tracking records to delete.

200.description: The video tracking data was deleted successfully.

400.description: The input model is null.

post.summary: Deletes user content security records for a user, content item, content definition, or combination thereof.
post.description: Behavior: Removes content security records based on the provided filter criteria. Supports filtering by user ID, content ID, content definition ID, and content security attribute, which can be combined to target specific records. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes matching user content security records. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the input model is null.

requestBody.description: The filter criteria specifying which content security records to delete.

200.description: The user content security data was deleted successfully.

400.description: The input model is null.

delete.summary: Deletes all saved searches for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all saved search records belonging to the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's saved searches. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all saved search records for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's saved searches.

200.description: The saved searches were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all user session data for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all session records belonging to the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's sessions. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all user session records. Active sessions will be invalidated.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's sessions.

200.description: The user sessions were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all content groups owned by a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all content groups created by the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's content groups. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all content groups owned by the user, including their content associations and sharing records.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's content groups.

200.description: The content groups were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all content attributes (favorites, bookmarks, likes) for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all user content attribute records for the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's attributes. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all user content attribute records.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's content attributes.

200.description: The content attributes were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all favorite records for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all content items marked as favorites by the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's favorites. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all favorite records for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's favorites.

200.description: The favorites were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all like records for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all content items marked as liked by the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's likes. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all like records for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's likes.

200.description: The likes were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all dislike records for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all content items marked as disliked by the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's dislikes. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all dislike records for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's dislikes.

200.description: The dislikes were deleted successfully.

delete.summary: Deletes all share records for a user.
delete.description: Behavior: Removes all content sharing records for the specified user. If no user ID is provided, operates on the current user's shares. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token. Side Effects: Permanently deletes all share records for the user.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: Optional user ID. If null, targets the current user's shares.

200.description: The shares were deleted successfully.

post.summary: Receives a batch of behavior events from the Angular client.
post.description: Behavior: Accepts a batch of user behavior events (actions, navigation, interactions) from the Nomad Angular UI. Events are validated, normalized, and stored in ClickHouse for eventual aggregation into user profiles. Supports both regular HTTP requests with Bearer token and sendBeacon requests with body-borne beacon token. Authorization: Regular requests require Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Beacon requests (navigator.sendBeacon) use beaconToken in request body since custom headers cannot be set. Side Effects: Stores events in ClickHouse. Events are immutable once written (append-only). Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if batch envelope is malformed. Returns 403 if event userId doesn't match authenticated user. Returns 413 if batch exceeds size limits. Returns 207 if some events are rejected. Returns 202 for full success.

requestBody.description: The event batch containing events, viewport info, and optional beacon auth.

202.description: All events accepted and queued for processing.

207.description: Some events accepted, some rejected. See errors array.

400.description: Batch envelope is malformed.

403.description: Event userId doesn't match authenticated user.

413.description: Batch exceeds max size (100 events or 256KB).

get.summary: Returns raw events for debugging and validation (admin only).
get.description: Behavior: Returns raw, un-aggregated behavior events for a specific user. This is an admin/debug endpoint, not for production AI consumption. Authorization: Requires admin user. Checks internally via CoreHelper. Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user ID (required).

parameters.name: startTime
parameters.description: ISO 8601 start of window (optional).

parameters.name: endTime
parameters.description: ISO 8601 end of window (optional).

parameters.name: eventType
parameters.description: Filter: "action", "navigation", or "interaction" (optional).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max results. Default: 100, Max: 500.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset. Default: 0.

200.description: Returns the raw events.

400.description: Missing required userId parameter.

403.description: The caller is not authorized (admin required).

get.summary: Returns an aggregated behavior profile for a specific user.
get.description: Behavior: Returns aggregated user behavior data (widget usage, page patterns, navigation flows, etc.) for the specified user and time window. This is the primary read endpoint for the AI system that generates custom UIs. Authorization: Requires admin user. Checks internally via CoreHelper. Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 403 if user is not an admin. Returns 404 if no profile exists for the user/window combination.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The user ID.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window: "7d", "30d", or "90d". Default: "30d".

parameters.name: granularity
parameters.description: Granularity: "summary" or "daily". Default: "summary".

200.description: Returns the user behavior profile.

403.description: The caller is not authorized (admin required).

404.description: No profile found for this user/window.

get.summary: Returns behavior profiles for all users in an organization (for bulk AI processing).
get.description: Behavior: Returns a paginated list of user behavior profiles for an organization. Used by the AI system for bulk processing and analysis. Authorization: Requires admin user. Checks internally via CoreHelper. Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: The organization ID (required).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Time window: "7d", "30d", or "90d". Default: "30d".

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Pagination limit. Default: 50, Max: 200.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Pagination offset. Default: 0.

parameters.name: minSessions
parameters.description: Only include users with at least N sessions. Default: 1.

200.description: Returns the list of profiles.

400.description: Missing required organizationId parameter.

403.description: The caller is not authorized (admin required).

get.summary: Returns the widget registry (vocabulary of available widgets).
get.description: Behavior: Returns the canonical widget registry. When organizationId is supplied, the registry is filtered by the org's enabled feature flags so the AI never proposes widgets the org can't render (e.g., live channels disabled, chat disabled). Authorization: Requires admin user. Checks internally via CoreHelper. Side Effects: None.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description: Optional. Filter the registry by the org's enabled features.

200.description: Returns the widget registry.

403.description: The caller is not authorized (admin required).

get.summary: Get all active user sessions, offline shared-access users, and pending invitations for a live channel.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the list of users associated with a live channel, organized into two groups: Items contains currently online users with active sessions, and RelatedItems contains offline shared-access users (with " (offline)" suffix on LastName) and users with pending invitations. Currently only supports live channel content definitions — other content types return an empty result. The authenticated user is always included in the online results. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns an empty result if the id does not match a supported live channel content definition. Returns 404 if the session data cannot be retrieved.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Optional. Must be the LiveChannelContentDefinitionId (LVEP) to return results. Can also be a UserSessionId, UserId, ContentId, or ContentDefinitionId but only LVEP is currently supported.

200.description: Returns the session list. Items = online users, RelatedItems = offline + pending users.

404.description: Session data could not be retrieved.

get.summary: Retrieve a single user session by its ID.
get.description: Behavior: Returns the details of a specific user session including user information, session metadata (browser type, IP address, start and last activity dates), and current status. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: None. Error Scenarios: Returns 404 if no session exists with the specified ID.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier (Guid) of the user session to retrieve.

200.description: Returns the user session details.

404.description: No session was found with the specified ID.

post.summary: Change the status of a user session (e.g., kick a user or restrict chat access).
post.description: Behavior: Updates the status of the specified user session to a new value such as Kicked, ChatRestricted, or Expired. This is commonly used to kick a user off the system or restrict their chat privileges. The affected user will see the status change on their next session ping. Authorization: Requires a valid Bearer token (class-level [Authorize]). Side Effects: Changes the session status. The affected user will see the status change on their next ping. Error Scenarios: Returns 400 if the request is null or Id/UserSessionStatus are missing.

requestBody.description: A Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.UserSession.ChangeSessionStatusModel containing the session Id (required), UserSessionStatus to set (required), and optional ApplicationId.

200.description: The session status was successfully changed. Returns the updated session.

400.description: The request is null, Id is missing, or UserSessionStatus is invalid.

token.description: Gets or sets the JWT access token. Include this in the Authorization header of all API requests, prefixed with "Bearer ".

refreshToken.description: Gets or sets the refresh token. Use this to obtain a new access token when the current one expires.

expirationSeconds.description: Gets or sets the duration in seconds until the access token expires.

Gets or sets the full S3 object key for a single-file undelete, or the folder prefix

versionId.description:
Gets or sets the version ID of the S3 delete marker to remove.
Required when Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.IsFolder is `false`; ignored when `true`.

isFolder.description:
Gets or sets a value indicating whether to undelete all items under the given
Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.ObjectKey prefix (folder mode) rather than a single specific file.

UndeleteRequestModel.description: Request payload for the undelete endpoint. Supports two modes: Single-file undelete — set Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.IsFolder to `false` and supply both Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.ObjectKey and Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.VersionId (the delete-marker version ID returned by the list endpoint). Only that specific delete marker is removed. Folder undelete — set Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.IsFolder to `true` and supply Nomad.Common.Models.Admin.Asset.UndeleteRequestModel.ObjectKey as the folder prefix (e.g. "Content/Videos/"). Every delete marker whose key begins with that prefix is removed.

tags.name: AnonymousUpload

Public (no authentication required) controller for the anonymous video upload workflow.
Implements a 4-step submission process: email → token verification → upload → confirmation.

content definitions into logical categories for the admin UI.

content rendering, rank sequence updates, and hierarchical instance management.

tags.description:
Provides administrative endpoints for retrieving content types, which define the categories
of content that can be managed in the system.

tags.description:
Provides administrative endpoints for retrieving field definitions, which define the available
field types that can be used when configuring content definitions.

tags.description:
Controller for the Live Schedule API used by content items that participate in
automated live channel broadcasting. The content definition must have its

content definition URLs, and other system reference data.
